title: eriosema spicatum
intro: Eriosema spicatum, also known as the hairy stickpea, is a perennial legume native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Eriosema spicatum include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Eriosema spicatum can be made by drying the leaves and roots and using them in infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture.
